original_image_tags: The reverse side of a yellowish, aged document page. The paper shows several stamped impressions, some appearing upside down or backwards, indicating they are from the other side of the page bleeding through. There are two hole-punch remnants at the top edge. The main body of text is faint and reversed (bleed-through from the front).
A faint, pinkish-red stamp impression. Text is partially visible but reversed and faded.
A dark blue/black stamp impression, oriented vertically.
A red rectangular stamp, oriented correctly.

elements.description: Faint, aged paper surface containing a single red rectangular ink stamp on the right side with legible text 'SEPT 1 - 1930' and 'FILED 30'. Multiple overlapping blue and green ink stamps are present in the center, bottom, and left margin, featuring the text 'FBI' and various dates from 1959. Faint reversed (bleed-through) typed text is visible across the page, but no photographs, sketches, or diagrams are present.

judge.reason: Description B quotes specific verbatim stamp text ('SEPT 1 - 1930', 'FILED 30', 'FBI') that Description A does not mention at all, creating a direct contradiction on the presence of legible text content.
